title_original: New Mexico bets big on emerging quantum computing industry
summary_original: New Mexico is intensifying its efforts in quantum computing, with state lawmakers approving over $100 million in incentives in 2024 to attract quantum companies such as Qunnect, which recently launched the first open-ended quantum network in Albuquerque. Key institutions include Los Alamos and Sandia National Laboratories and the University of New Mexico's Quantum Institute, described as one of the most highly regarded research programs in the United States. The newly formed Quantum New Mexico Institute, led by Robert Ledoux, is central to consolidating resources. In 2024, New Mexico and Colorado became the first federally designated quantum hub. Leaders anticipate the industry will create hundreds to potentially thousands of jobs in the near future and cite projections of a $1 trillion quantum sector by 2035.
